untranslatable_reason: Diamond Top pattern recognition requires visual/shape detection of price action (diamond formation at market top with specific geometry). This cannot be expressed using only the allowed technical indicators and comparison operators. The pattern requires identifying: (1) a specific multi-leg price formation shape, (2) precise breakout direction determination from that shape, and (3) context-dependent bullish/bearish outcomes. No combination of available indicators (moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, etc.) can reliably detect the diamond geometric pattern itself. The author provides outcome statistics (69% success in bear market, 17% average decline, 43-81 day timeframe) but no mechanical rules for identifying diamond formations.
translation_notes: The Diamond Top pattern is inherently a candlestick/price-action pattern that requires shape recognition. While entry/exit rules were attempted using pivot breakouts as a proxy, this does not capture the actual diamond formation logic described in Bulkowski's work. A proper implementation would require either: (1) custom pattern recognition code, (2) fractals or zigzag indicators to identify peaks/valleys, or (3) manual chart pattern annotation. The 43-81 day window and market regime context are preserved in the hold_days_max and target_regime fields.
